inputs.label: Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure of the hydraulic or pneumatic system. Typical range: 1-350 bar for hydraulics, 1-10 bar for pneumatics. Based on ISO 4414 (pneumatics) and ISO 4413 (hydraulics).

inputs.label: Piston Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of the cylinder piston. Standard sizes per ISO 3320 (hydraulic/pneumatic cylinders).

inputs.label: Rod Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of the piston rod. Affects effective area on retraction stroke. Standard sizes per ISO 3320.

inputs.label: Cylinder Type
inputs.businessContext: Single-acting cylinders use pressure on one side only; double-acting use pressure on both sides. Common in industrial applications per ISO 6020/6022.

inputs.label: Fluid Type
inputs.businessContext: Hydraulic uses incompressible oil; pneumatic uses compressible air. Affects force calculation (pneumatic may have pressure drop due to compressibility).

inputs.label: Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Mechanical efficiency accounting for friction losses. Typical 80-95% for hydraulic cylinders, 70-90% for pneumatic. Based on manufacturer data and ISO 4391.

rules: rodDiameter must be less than pistonDiameter
if cylinderType is 'single-acting', rodDiameter is not used in extension force calculation

thresholds.pressure: if pressure > 350 bar for hydraulic or > 10 bar for pneumatic, warning: 'Pressure exceeds typical range'
thresholds.efficiency: if efficiency < 70, warning: 'Low efficiency may indicate wear or misalignment'

formulas.primary: if cylinderType == 'double-acting' then forceExtension else forceExtension (single-acting extension only)

hiddenLossDrivers: if pressure > 350 and fluidType == 'hydraulic' then 'High pressure'
if pressure > 10 and fluidType == 'pneumatic' then 'High pressure'
suggestedActions: Check for seal wear if efficiency is low
Consider using a larger cylinder if force is insufficient
outputs.dataConfidenceAdjusted: forceExtension * (dataConfidence/100) if dataConfidence input exists else forceExtension
